comments.text: With the assistance of OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Pro, I have written up some partial results on this problem. The current manuscript and source are available here: https://github.com/lsngchl/Erdos432 Let \(A,B\subseteq\mathbb N\) be infinite, and suppose that the distinct elements of \(A+B\) are pairwise coprime. Write\[ S_{A,B}(x):=|(A+B)\cap[1,x]|. \]1. Global upper bound. For every such pair \(A,B\),\[ S_{A,B}(x)\le \pi(x) \]for all \(x\ge 1\). This is a simple observation. 2. Uniform lower bound. There exist infinite sets \(A,B\subseteq\mathbb N\) satisfying the pairwise-coprimality condition such that, for all sufficiently large \(x\),\[ S_{A,B}(x)\gg \left(\frac{\log x}{\log\log x}\right)^2. \]3. Limsup lower bound. For every function \(F(x)=x^{o(1)}\), there exist infinite sets \(A,B\subseteq\mathbb N\) satisfying the pairwise-coprimality condition and a sequence \(x_j\to\infty\) such that\[ S_{A,B}(x_j)\ge F(x_j). \]4. Conditional limsup lower bound. Assuming the Hardy--Littlewood prime-tuples conjecture, for every function \(\omega(x)\to\infty\) there exist infinite sets \(A,B\subseteq\mathbb N\) such that all sums \(a+b\) are prime and distinct, and there is a sequence \(x_j\to\infty\) such that\[ S_{A,B}(x_j)\ge \frac{x_j}{(\log x_j)^{\omega(x_j)}}. \]
